title: GS Paper II
subtitle: Governance, Constitution, Polity, Social Justice and International Relations

items.text: Historical Evolution, Constituent Assembly, and Objectives of the Constitution
Salient Features of the Indian Constitution, Unitary and Federal Features
Preamble (Key terminologies: Sovereign, Socialist, Secular, Democratic, Republic, Justice, Liberty)
Citizenship (Provisions, OCI, PIO, Dual citizenship concepts)
Fundamental Rights (Significance, Writs, Limitations, Article 32)
Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP) and Fundamental Duties
Amendment of the Constitution & Basic Structure Doctrine (Evolution, Cases)

topics.name: Organs of State & Separation of Powers

items.text: Separation of Powers, Checks and Balances in the Indian Constitution vs US/UK
The Executive (President, Vice-President, Prime Minister, Council of Ministers, Attorney General)
Parliament and State Legislatures (Structure, Conduct of Business, Powers, Privileges, Dissolution)
The Judiciary (Integrated Judicial System, Supreme Court, High Courts, Collegium System, Judicial Activism, PIL)
Local Government (73rd and 74th Constitutional Amendment Acts, Devolution of Finances, Issues)
Constitutional Bodies (Election Commission, UPSC, SPSC, Finance Commission, GST Council, CAG)
Non-Constitutional Bodies (NITI Aayog, NHRC, CVC, CBI, Lokpal and Lokayuktas)
Statutory, Regulatory and various Quasi-Judicial Bodies (SEBI, IRDA, Tribunal, NCLAT)
Government Interventions for Development, Good Governance, Transparency
Development Processes and Industry (Role of NGOs, SHGs, Donor agencies, Charities)
Citizen Charters, Transparency, Accountability, and Institutional Measures (Ombudsman, Whistleblowers)
e-Governance (Applications, Models, Successes, Limitations, Future prospects)
Role of Civil Services in a Democracy (Policy formulation, Implementation, Neutrality, Reforms)
Welfare Schemes for Vulnerable Sections (SC, ST, OBC, Women, Children, Disabled, Migrants)
Services relating to Health, Education, and Human Resources development
Issues relating to Poverty, Hunger, Malnutrition, and Unemployment (National Food Security Act)
Determinants of Foreign Policy, Non-Aligned Movement (NAM), Panchsheel, Nuclear Doctrine
Bilateral relations with Neighbours (Indo-Pak, Indo-China, India-Nepal, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, Maldives)
Indian Diaspora (Role played, Safety, Schemes for Overseas Indians)
Bilateral Relations with Major Powers (Indo-USA, Indo-Russia, Indo-Japan, Indo-Europe, Indo-Africa)
Regional & Global Groupings (QUAD, BRICS, BIMSTEC, G20, ASEAN, G7, Shanghai Cooperation Organisation)
Global Institutions (United Nations, WTO, IMF, World Bank, WHO, International Court of Justice)
